deepseek-r1-distill-llama-8b.text: Okay, so I'm trying to figure out the diagnosis for this 27-year-old man.

Next, there are murmurs heard over the ribs on both sides anteriorly and over the back posteriorly.

Claudication with exertion suggests he has trouble walking, which is often seen in peripheral artery disease, but the other findings point more towards aortic issues.
Putting it all together, the aortic ejection click and the murmurs are key.
The feeble pulses and claudication support the idea of reduced blood flow through the aorta, possibly due to a narrowing or aneurysm.
So, the most likely diagnosis is aortic stenosis or a related aortic condition.
